data.body:
PLAN REVIEW · PASS 1 · [T8] LLM/prompt structural-completeness probe (overlay) — VERDICT: CLEAN (no findings).

This epic defines an LLM/agent system (3 agentic reviewers + eval specs + deterministic-scorer registry + a context-injection live solver), so the T8 overlay applies. I tool-probed each structural-gap class against the codebase and found every one satisfied at the plan level:

- (a) No undefined-but-referenced enum/schema vocabulary. The "deterministic-scorer registry" AC requires every scorer NAME used by the packaged specs to map to an implemented pure function. The scorer-name vocabulary is concretely defined: existing packaged specs name them (src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-quality.eval.yaml:19,22 emits_valid_review_result/cites_real_paths; plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:19,22,27; plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml:16,19,26; plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml:15,18,21), and the 3 NEW specs' scorer names are an explicit authoring deliverable (child rink-cloud-gum Phase-1 + Phase-2.1). The scorer TYPE enum (deterministic | llm-judge) is fully defined and enforced in validate_scorer (src/rebar/llm/eval.py:120-166). This is a build deliverable, not a referenced-but-undefined enum.

- (b) Processing protocol co-located with the schema that needs it. The grader-discipline decision rules (cross-family judge, gates:false for judges, at_least(k) over epochs, coverage threshold, pinned/dated grader) are co-located with the spec-validation surface they govern (validate_eval_spec / validate_scorer / parse_gate, eval.py:107-223). The AC mirrors these rules exactly (cross-family judge gates:false, at_least(k) over explicit epochs, coverage threshold, non-empty gold_set), so the executor's rule and the schema are not split.

- (c) No counter/state increment with ambiguous placement. at_least(k)-over-epochs (eval.py:229-231) and coverage = scored/total (eval.py:234-240) are precisely defined; the unsatisfiable-k guard (eval.py:207-208) closes the only edge.

- (d) A fallback for an incomplete/failed sub-step is specified. The agentic reviewers carry an explicit failed-sub-step fallback in-prompt ("after a bounded search, you cannot find evidence ... treat it as NOT MET — never assume", reviewers/completion_verifier.md:88-89), and the eval AC names BOTH branches of the repo-tool fallback ("stubbed OR fixture-backed repo tools"). The deeper "what if a reviewer tool call escapes the fixture corpus" detail is reasonably deferred to the task child (rink-cloud-gum Phase-2.2/2.4), not an under-specification of the plan criterion itself.

- (e) No instruction-locality/pink-elephant antipattern; referenced agents/skills exist and are fully specified (tool-verified). The 3 agentic reviewers the eval seam targets all exist and are fully specified — src/rebar/llm/reviewers/completion_verifier.md (untrusted-input guard, bounded tool budget, structured completion_verdict, read-only constraints), ticket_quality.md, spec_alignment.md. run_eval()'s NotImplementedError stub is correctly characterized by the plan as an unwired-but-present harness (eval.py:319-351), matching the AC's "no longer raises NotImplementedError" deliverable.

affirmations: [T8] — no undefined-but-referenced scorer/enum vocabulary; grader-discipline protocol co-located with the spec schema; at_least(k)/coverage counters unambiguously placed; failed-sub-step fallback specified (reviewer NOT-MET default + stubbed/fixture repo-tool branch); referenced agentic reviewers exist and are fully specified (tool-verified). Empty findings list for T8 — clean chunk.
